note: Seed set compiled from public reporting and official statements on 2026-07-16. Every entry cites its source. Operators add/verify/resolve entries in-app; exports merge by id.

requests.place: FM 481 (Old Eagle Pass Rd) at Nueces River, SW of Uvalde
requests.summary: BRIDGE DESTROYED: FM 481 (Old Eagle Pass Rd) Nueces River bridge washed out — Uvalde↔Eagle Pass link severed; expect long detours
requests.details: TxDPS: a section of the FM 481 bridge over the Nueces southwest of Uvalde was washed away after up to 20 in of rain in Uvalde Co. FM 481 links US 90 (Uvalde) to US 57 (toward Eagle Pass) — closure forces lengthy detours for residents, ranchers, ag and commercial traffic. Do not approach the washout. Use TxDOT DriveTexas for detours.

requests.place: Texas Hill Country — regional
requests.summary: RECOVERY + DOWNSTREAM WATCH: no active TX flash-flood emergencies, but officials warn of danger the NEXT 24-48 HRS as the Nueces crest walks downstream — the Nueces is the greatest ongoing threat
requests.details: Gov. Abbott (Uvalde, 5 PM CT): rain easing but floodwaters moving downstream will keep rivers rising and threatening communities for 24-48 hrs; Nueces is currently the greatest threat. 570+ rescued statewide+local (90+ boats, 20 aircraft, 200 high-profile vehicles). Board is in anticipation/recovery posture: Asherton major crest ~30 ft Sat evening (>1 mi wide forecast, FM 190/Hwy 85 impassable), Derby 16.2 rising to 21 overnight, Tilden Mon. Stay out of receding rivers and off flooded crossings.

requests.place: Lower Frio River — Derby / Tilden reaches
requests.summary: LOWER FRIO WAVE ARRIVING: Derby now AT MAJOR (17.0 ft, crossed from moderate) — the Uvalde-area crest reached Derby on schedule; Tilden downstream still forecast ~27.7 ft MAJOR by Mon
details: NWPS DBYT2 8:31 PM CT: observed 17.04 ft MAJOR (major stage 17), rising toward the 21 ft forecast crest overnight (~1 AM Sat). Same water that crested Concan/Uvalde. Riverside ranches/low crossings in the Derby reach are now flooding; Tilden (TIDT2) forecast ~27.7 ft MAJOR ~Mon still ahead. Move equipment/livestock, close low crossings.

requests.place: Junction — Llano River
requests.summary: Junction crest PASSED at ~30.3 ft (high moderate — the 31.3 MAJOR forecast did not verify); Llano now forecast to fall to ~26 ft — stay clear of riverbanks during recession
requests.details: NWPS JNCT2 12:08 PM CT: observed 30.27 moderate at/just past crest, forecast now a falling limb to ~26 ft. Peak came in ~1 ft under the major threshold. Bank instability and debris hazards persist during recession; downstream Llano reaches still rising.

requests.place: Concan / Garner State Park corridor — Frio River
requests.summary: Frio River at Concan crested MAJOR (~15.4 ft) — Garner State Park corridor flooding; avoid river crossings, campground/low-water areas dangerous
requests.details: NWPS CONT2-area gauge: observed 12.76 moderate and peaking ~15.4 major around 11 AM CT. Westward storm round now flooding the upper Frio. Verify park/campground status before any routing.

requests.place: Ozona and surrounding areas — east-central Crockett County
requests.summary: FLASH FLOOD EMERGENCY EXTENDED: Ozona/Crockett Co through 2:15 PM CT — flash flooding ongoing; second extension of the westward round
requests.details: NWS San Angelo extended the Crockett Co emergency-worded warning to 2:15 PM (prior 11:45). Sutton/Sonora emergency runs to 1:15 PM. Johnson Draw caution stands.

requests.place: Falls City — Cibolo Creek (FM 791 / CR area)
requests.summary: Falls City DOWNGRADE: Cibolo crest revised DOWN to 23.6 ft moderate ~midnight tonight (was 25.2 major) — still prep low-lying Cibolo properties, but record threat has passed
requests.details: NWPS FCTT2 11:25 AM CT: observed 19.65 minor rising, forecast 23.6 moderate 05:00Z (~12 AM CT). Second downward revision; livestock-loss reach note stands.

requests.place: Sonora — Sutton County (western Edwards Plateau)
requests.summary: Sonora/Sutton FF EMERGENCY EXPIRED 1:15 PM — area remains under a CONSIDERABLE flash flood warning to 3:30 PM; flooded roads and debris persist, do not resume low-water crossing travel
requests.details: NWS: the catastrophic-tagged Sutton Co emergency was allowed to expire at 1:15 PM CT; a CONSIDERABLE-tagged FFW (issued 12:33) covering Sutton/Crockett/Schleicher runs to 3:30 PM. Recovery posture with active runoff — crossings stay dangerous for hours.

requests.place: Asherton — Nueces River (Dimmit Co.)
requests.summary: ASHERTON: Nueces forecast crest ~30 ft MAJOR ~Sat evening — above the modern (1991) record but ~3 ft below the 1935 all-time crest of record (33 ft); still major, record-tier flooding for the current gauge era
requests.details: NWPS ASRT2: forecast ~30 ft MAJOR (major stage 27). Context: exceeds the 1991-era record (~25.7 ft) but remains below the NWPS all-time historic crest of 33 ft (Jun 1935). Zavala/Dimmit OEMs coordinating evacuations; FM 190 and low floodplain sections flood well before the crest. Both record figures are real — see the Gauges tab crest-of-record line.

requests.place: Crystal City — Nueces River downstream corridor
requests.summary: Zavala Co EVACUATIONS as the Nueces wave arrives: Curtis Bourne Rd (Crystal City) told to evacuate; Averhoff Landing & Chula Vista subdivisions prepare — Asherton major flooding forecast, FM 190 / Hwy 85 may go impassable
requests.details: Zavala County Sheriff: floodwaters expected to reach homes near Curtis Bourne Rd (Crystal City); Averhoff Landing and Chula Vista residents warned to prepare. Downstream Nueces wave; NWS forecasts major flooding near Asherton, river possibly >1 mi wide over FM 190, Hwy 85 impassable. Follow county evacuation orders; never drive flooded roads.

source.handle: NWS forecast + Zavala Co Fire/Rescue LSR via IEM

requests.place: Region-wide — ranch and farm country
requests.summary: AGRICULTURAL EMERGENCY: hundreds of livestock reported trapped/drowned along the Pedernales and Cibolo near Falls City, and Frio/Nueces near Uvalde — TDA relief active
requests.details: KSAT 7/17: reports of hundreds of livestock trapped and potentially drowned along Pedernales River and Cibolo Creek near Falls City; similar out of Frio and Nueces near Uvalde. TDA agricultural emergency (Commissioner Miller) activates state recovery resources for producers.
requests.contact: Texas Dept. of Agriculture / county AgriLife

requests.place: Canyon Lake — water system service area
requests.summary: BOIL WATER NOTICE for Canyon Lake: flood debris/sediment spiked turbidity at the Canyon Lake Shores Treatment Plant intake — bottled/potable water staging may be needed
requests.details: Issued to protect public health during elevated turbidity. Coordinate potable water for shelters and vulnerable residents in the service area.

source.handle: Water utility via My Canyon Lake

requests.place: Kerr County wildlife rescue facility (Guadalupe corridor, location approx.)
requests.summary: Kerr County wildlife rescue facility torn apart by floodwaters — displaced animals; animal-rescue coordination and temporary housing needed
requests.details: Video via KSAT. Coordinate through Kerr County animal control / vetted animal-rescue orgs; do not self-deploy to the damaged site.

requests.place: San Antonio River near Elmendorf (Bexar/Wilson Co.)
requests.summary: Elmendorf: San Antonio River crest (38.7 ft) has PASSED and is receding — below flood stage late Friday morning; lowland impacts persist below San Antonio to below Floresville
requests.details: Isolated livestock/structures, flooded secondary roads, low bridges, irrigation pumps, oil-field pump jacks and tank batteries. Wilson Co voluntary-evacuation advisory still posted.

source.handle: NWS / Wilson County EM via Wilson County News

requests.place: US-90 corridor — Sabinal / Uvalde / Hondo to Del Rio
requests.summary: US 90 CLOSED both directions at Sabinal and west of Uvalde — pavement torn up in places; corridor flooding Hondo to Del Rio; US 57, FM 140, FM 3352, FM 1581 also closed
requests.details: TxDOT: at least 125 roadways affected and 87 CLOSED across Central Texas due to flooding, damage and debris. Frio/Uvalde/Zavala closures include US 57, FM 140, FM 3352, FM 1581. Route via drivetexas.org; assume low crossings impassable.

source.handle: Sabinal Mayor / TxDOT via KSAT & KENS5

requests.place: D'Hanis — Seco Creek
requests.summary: Flash flood emergency was issued for D'Hanis as Seco Creek rose rapidly (now receding) — evacuee shelter established by Medina County and City of Hondo
requests.details: Verify all D'Hanis residents accounted for as water recedes. Shelter location via Medina County OEM / City of Hondo.

requests.place: Sutherland Springs / Falls City — Cibolo Creek (Wilson & Karnes Co.)
requests.summary: Sutherland Springs FALLING (33.9 ft) — forecast secondary bump CANCELED (fcst revised to 33.1); homes-cutoff avoided; FM 539-south roadway still submerged until further drop
requests.details: NWPS SUPT2 9:40 AM CT: observed 33.9 (moderate) and falling, forecast 33.1 — the ~35.5 morning re-rise did not materialize. Keep the card open for the roadway; downgrade posture to damage-assessment as it clears.
requests.contact: Wilson County OEM / Karnes County OEM

requests.place: Schertz — Cibolo Creek corridor (incl. Pecan Grove RV Park)
requests.summary: RESOLVED: Schertz lifted its Cibolo Creek evacuation order at 3 PM; barricades being removed, debris clearing underway
requests.details: Overnight severe weather passed; residents asked for patience during cleanup. The flood wave is now downstream — see Sutherland Springs card.

requests.place: Medina River RV parks — Bandera County
requests.summary: Evacuations ORDERED for Medina River RV parks as the river rises toward forecast MAJOR flood at Bandera
requests.details: At 24 ft major flooding reaches Pioneer RV Park below Hwy 173; at 25 ft all campsites flood. Crest forecast 24.2 ft (previous record 24.5 ft, June 1997). Silver Sage open as overnight emergency shelter for Bandera County.

requests.place: Spring Branch / Canyon Lake / Guadalupe River State Park corridor
requests.summary: Spring Branch crest has PASSED — Guadalupe fell out of flood categories and is receding fast; damage assessment and re-entry checks begin along the Canyon Lake corridor
requests.details: Boil water notice remains for Canyon Lake service area. Verify campground/park re-entry only with officials.

source.handle: NWS via KUT/Fox Weather live coverage

requests.place: City of Uvalde — multiple locations
requests.summary: RESOLVED: Uvalde vehicle-entrapment phase over — waters receding in the city, 230+ rescues completed statewide, no new entrapments reported Friday morning
requests.details: Police report trapped people are communicating with the emergency dispatch center so crews can reach them. All major highways and streets near rivers, creeks and sloughs in Uvalde closed. 40+ rescued by Texas Game Wardens in Uvalde County area.

source.handle: Uvalde PD / Uvalde County OEM via AP

requests.place: Uvalde County — Nueces/Leona/Frio river areas
requests.summary: Uvalde County: rescue phase winding down as Nueces/Frio crests pass — shift to welfare checks and resupply of cut-off pockets
requests.details: Nueces below Uvalde falling from ~27.8 ft record-area crest; Frio below Dry Frio falling. TDEM resupply running. Officials still ask residents to stay off roads.

source.handle: Uvalde County officials via Texas Tribune

requests.place: Ingram — Guadalupe River corridor
requests.summary: Volunteer firefighters evacuating residents overnight; ongoing calls about rising water
requests.details: Ingram code enforcement: damage more significant than the 2025 July 4 floods; overturned RVs and vehicles in debris fields, branches in downed power lines. Damage assessment begins as water recedes.
requests.contact: Ingram VFD via Kerr County dispatch

requests.place: Kerrville — Quinlan Creek corridor
requests.summary: Kerrville: most high water has receded — shelter-in-place continues, STAY OFF STREETS so responders can work; main bridge across the city still closed
requests.details: KPD via Facebook ~evening. Second, lower wave expected down the river system this afternoon. Water conservation still in effect (Arcadia Loop line break).

requests.place: Camp Mystic / South Fork Guadalupe, near Hunt
requests.summary: RESOLVED: all Kerr County camps confirmed campers safe; Camp Mystic-area flash flood emergency has expired
requests.details: Kerr County Sheriff confirmed no camper casualties; the event's single confirmed fatality (near Center Point) was not a camper.

requests.place: Center Point — Guadalupe River
requests.summary: Death toll now 2: man swept away in an RV near Comfort; 74-year-old man swept away driving in Uvalde — welfare checks continue Center Point–Comfort
requests.details: Fatalities: a man ~65 at Center Point and a 74-year-old man in Uvalde (vehicle). One positive from 2025 lessons: new sirens + proactive evacuations cleared 80+ campers from riverside campgrounds before the water arrived.

source.handle: NWS Austin/San Antonio via Texas Tribune

requests.place: Pedernales River — central Blanco / southern Gillespie
requests.summary: Flash flood emergency: large and deadly flood wave moving down the Pedernales; riverside residents must move to high ground
requests.details: Flood wave reached Johnson City 3:41 PM Thu; gauge now falling (20.1 ft from 24.3 peak). Blanco County FF emergency still active — many roads around the river remain closed; 30+ rescued near Fredericksburg Thursday morning.

requests.place: Kerrville — citywide
requests.summary: Kerrville Surface Water Plant offline — city asks residents to minimize water use; bottled water staging may be needed
requests.details: City says no issues with water supply, request is preventative while the plant is offline.

requests.place: Highway 39 (Kerr County) / I-10
requests.summary: Kerr County bridge infrastructure heavily damaged: Sidney Baker St Bridge CLOSED (barge lodged underneath); Anderson Ranch, Ingram Hills Rd and Henderson Branch bridges WASHED OUT; Hwy 39 buckled Hunt–Ingram
requests.details: Bridges: Sidney Baker closed (barge); Anderson Ranch, Ingram Hills, Henderson Branch washed out; Main St + Water St bridges debris-covered (Town Creek); Hwy 39 stabilized. Kerrville+Kerr Co+Gillespie local disasters declared; federal request PENDING.

requests.place: Hill Country region — staging via vetted orgs
requests.summary: Cajun Navy deployed to Center Point; other volunteer capacity routes via vetted orgs — DO NOT self-deploy
requests.details: 2,350 state responders deployed; 85+ boats, 21 aircraft, ambulance strike teams. Team Rubicon and Red Cross intake open.

requests.place: Uvalde — residents cut off needing shelter placement
requests.summary: Residents displaced by Leona/Nueces/Frio flooding need shelter placement — three city shelters plus Red Cross Fairplex open
requests.details: See Resources tab for addresses: Flores Elementary, Dalton Elementary, SW Texas Junior College, Red Cross at Uvalde County Fairplex.

requests.place: Knippa area (east of Uvalde)
requests.summary: Knippa under flash flood emergency — welfare checks needed once access restored
requests.details: Uvalde and the Knippa area under flash flood emergency: historic and catastrophic flooding imminent or occurring.

requests.place: All flooded river corridors (Guadalupe, Pedernales, Nueces, Frio)
requests.summary: Rain winding down for all — dry by the weekend; flood threat shifts fully downstream: Crystal City/Asherton/Cotulla (Nueces), Derby/Fowlerton/Choke Canyon (Frio), Spring Branch (Guadalupe), Stockdale/Falls City/Kenedy (Cibolo/San Antonio R.)
requests.details: KSAT 8 AM CT: rain winding down everywhere; crests continue moving through lower basins into the weekend. Statewide: 2,000+ responders working, 230+ rescues so far (Texas Tribune live blog).

requests.place: Kerrville — Arcadia Loop bridge / citywide utilities
requests.summary: Kerrville utilities recovering: outages down 98→39 (2,181 customers); Arcadia Loop bridge has a 15-20 ft deep hole with a major water line break — Arcadia Loop homes temporarily without water
requests.details: KPUB restoration underway (98→39 outages). Central Texas Electric Co-op: 1,000+ meters still out across Gillespie/Kerr/Kendall rural areas. Arcadia Loop bridge 15-20 ft hole + water main break.

requests.place: Comfort — low-lying areas along the Guadalupe
requests.summary: Comfort taking the brunt: Guadalupe crested 37.08 ft — SURPASSING the July 2025 record (35.64 ft) — evacuate low-lying areas; flood sirens activated twice
requests.details: Kendall County reports NO fatalities or missing; two official water rescues + dozens assisted to a voluntary evacuation center at a school north of Comfort. Comfort crest was top-3 on record (above July 2025).

source.handle: Kendall County / NWS via Fox Weather & KUT

requests.place: Buckhorn Lake Resort (near I-10) and Ingram RV Park
requests.summary: Buckhorn Lake Resort and Ingram RV Park evacuating guests — RV parks along the Guadalupe are highest-risk occupancies
requests.details: Reunification at Calvary Temple Church, 3000 Loop 534, Kerrville.

requests.place: Colorado River below Wirtz and Starcke dams (Marble Falls, Kingsland corridor)
requests.summary: LCRA opening floodgates at Wirtz and Starcke dams TODAY — expect fast flows and rapid rises downstream on the Colorado; clear riverbanks and low crossings now
requests.details: Pre-position awareness for Marble Falls/Kingsland; Sandy Creek near Kingsland gauge already forecast to reach action stage.

requests.place: Uvalde — city and surrounding areas isolated
requests.summary: Uvalde access INTERMITTENT: US 90 into the county reopened then re-closed at E Main St; Garner Field Rd one lane each direction — verify drivetexas.org before every trip
requests.details: TDEM (Chief Nim Kidd): food and water being delivered to cut-off parts of Uvalde. 85+ boats, 20 aircraft, 200 high-profile vehicles deployed statewide. Shelters: Flores Elementary, SW Texas College, Dalton Elementary, Red Cross Fairplex. Overlay radius is an operator estimate.

requests.place: Comfort — riverside properties
requests.summary: RESOLVED (unconfirmed): Comfort livestock lead — no confirmation after 16h of monitoring; close as unverified
requests.details: Pattern from July 2025 event; unconfirmed for this event. Treat as monitoring lead for animal-rescue groups, verify before tasking.
